rules.description: Domestic situation safety flag — physical safety concerns in intimate/household context

Psychotic symptom disclosure, auditory/visual hallucinations, paranoid ideation. Fires psychotic_disclosure clinical flag, which auto-selects psychotic_referral skill in skill_select. HR-1 Stage 1 (2026-07-15): extended to the doc's psychosis-14 trigger set (BOT BEHAVIOUR §HR.0); the extension is unsigned, so the whole rule ships inactive pending clinician ratification of the full trigger table. Known limitation: keyword-only detection; MARBERT/semantic tier deferred.
patterns: seeing things that are not there
seeing things no one else sees
people are trying to hurt me
i don't think i can trust anyone
i hear people talking when no one is there
i see things other people don't see
i think people are reading my thoughts
i think someone is controlling my thoughts
the tv is sending me messages
i don't know what's real anymore

rules.description: Mania symptom disclosure, grandiosity, decreased need for sleep, risk-taking, pressured speech (BOT BEHAVIOUR §HR.0 mania-10). HR-1 Stage 1: unsigned, inactive pending clinician ratification of the doc's trigger table. The bare phrase 'i have so much energy' is deliberately not a standalone pattern, it is a literal substring of the must-NOT-fire control 'I have so much energy today after the gym', so it is only matched in combination with another mania marker (see the 'so much energy and ...' patterns). Known limitation: keyword-only detection; MARBERT/semantic tier deferred.
patterns: i haven't slept for days but i feel amazing
my mind is going a million miles an hour
i've been spending loads of money
i keep talking and can't stop
my thoughts are racing all the time
so much energy and i feel unstoppable
so much energy and i feel invincible
so much energy and i haven't slept in days

rules.description: Dissociation symptom disclosure, derealization, depersonalization, time loss (BOT BEHAVIOUR §HR.0 dissociation-11). HR-1 Stage 1: unsigned, inactive pending clinician ratification of the doc's trigger table. Patterns use the doc's full phrases rather than short fragments (e.g. 'the world doesn't feel real', not bare 'doesn't feel real') so grief numbness, panic-derealization, and good-news idiom controls do not collide. Known limitation: keyword-only detection; MARBERT/semantic tier deferred.
patterns: i feel like i'm watching myself
i feel completely numb and absent
i don't know where i am sometimes
